name: Guernsey (civil ensign)

colors.symbolism: the Union Flag canton and the inner cross
the gold cross of William the Conqueror

description: A British Red Ensign with the Union Flag in the canton, defaced in the fly with a yellow cross within a white cross — the Cross of William the Conqueror, taken from the Guernsey flag.
history: The civil ensign of Guernsey is a Red Ensign defaced with the gold-on-white Cross of William the Conqueror taken from the island's national flag. Adopted on 7 March 1985 by Royal Warrant.
